name: Bo Melton
headline: Full-time at receiver now, but the production isn't there
body:
Melton enters 2026 with a settled job description: LaFleur has committed him to wide receiver full-time after he spent prior stretches bouncing between offense and defensive back, and Green Bay's offseason departures at the position opened the door. That clarity is the only thing working in his favor right now. The projection sheet pegs him at roughly 3 catches, 38 yards and a fraction of a touchdown across a full slate, totaling around 8.6 fantasy points — bench-clinger territory.

The rankings back up the skepticism, with expert consensus near WR218 and dynasty value around WR197, and he sits in essentially zero leagues. His late-season tape was thin too: about 0.3 grabs per game over his final three weeks, with a 34-yard outing against Baltimore as the lone flicker. A friendly schedule doesn't matter without targets. Leave him on the wire until snaps and usage actually materialize.
